zh.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Southwell II 是Robert Southwell和Isabelle de Bois的兒子，於1449年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可武德利辛。他於1476年與Amy Witchingham在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾康寧斯比結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1514年9月27日在英國英格蘭藍卡爾利物浦艾華頓享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Southwell II 是Robert Southwell和Isabelle de Bois的儿子，于1449年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可武德利辛。他于1476年与Amy Witchingham在英国英格兰林可爾史爾康寧斯比结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1514年9月27日在英国英格兰藍卡爾利物浦艾華頓享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: Southwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Middle English south ‘south’ + wel(le) ‘well, pool, spring’ (Old English sūth + wella). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived ‘by the south well’ or ‘south of the well’, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Southwell (Nottinghamshire) or Southwell on the Isle of Portland (Dorset).
in Yorkshire and Lancashire, often a hypercorrect form of Soothill, a habitational name from Soothill in Dewsbury (Yorkshire), from Old English sōt ‘soot’ + hyll ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Southwell II  was born in 1449, in Woodrising, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom as the son of Robert Southwell and Isabelle de Bois. He married Amy Witchingham in 1476, in Coningsby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 27 September 1514, in Everton, Liverpool, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65.

Il a épousé Amy Witchingham en 1476, à Coningsby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Southwell II は1449年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクウッドリシングでRobert SouthwellとIsabelle de Boisの息子として生まれた。1476年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアコニングスビーでAmy Witchinghamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1514年9月27日にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアリバープオオルエバートンで65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Southwell II (은)는 1449년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 우드리징에서 Robert Southwell(와)과 Isabelle de Bois의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1476년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 코닝스비에서 Amy Witchingham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1514년 9월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 라이버포 에버턴에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Southwell II  nasceu em 1449, em Woodrising, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Robert Southwell e Isabelle de Bois. Ele casou-se com Amy Witchingham em 1476, em Coningsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 27 de setembro de 1514, em Everton, Liverpool, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 65 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Southwell II  родился в 1449 г. в местности вудрисинг, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали Robert Southwell и Isabelle de Bois. Он женился на женщине по имени Amy Witchingham в 1476 г. в местности конингсби, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 27 сентября 1514 г. в местности эвиртон, ливирпул, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Southwell II  nació en 1449, en Woodrising, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de Robert Southwell y Isabelle de Bois. Se casó con Amy Witchingham en 1476, en Coningsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 27 de septiembre de 1514, en Everton, Liverpool, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 65 años.
